programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man is garrotted beside a hotel pool surrounded by dozens of other sun-worshippers, yet none of them witnesses the attack because the killer struck during a solar eclipse, when all eyes were trained on the sky. Even more baffling, the first DNA evidence suggests a human-wolf mutation was present at the time of the attack

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. Kensi goes in search of her father's killer, before bumping into her mother, who she has not seen for more than 15 years. Meanwhile, the discovery that a member of Kensi's father's unit faked his own death prompts Granger to back down from his suspicions of his colleague. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Miguel Ferrer

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is trapped aboard an ocean liner when police officials board at sea to investigate the passengers

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: This episode begins in a Las Vegas gambling hall and ends on two slabs in the morgue, featuring a confusing case that involves three identical guns

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The hero goes undercover to bust a casino's money-laundering scheme, but is knocked unconscious. Walker is left running from the Mob - confused as to which side of the law he is on

programme.desc: Assistant coroner Palmer is called to the building site on a Navy base where a body has been found. While looking into the incident, he spots an armed man fleeing the scene with the victim's passport and is almost killed as he gives chase. It appears the document was stolen to hinder the investigation, but the discovery of a second corpse reveals a case of stolen identity and a compensation racket

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Vance sticks rigidly to the rules as he investigates the death of a fellow agent, but Gibbs and Franks take matters into their own hands to catch the killers. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. Under the guidance of therapist Dr Rachel Cranston, his memory begins to return and suspicion falls on his ex-lover EJ Barrett. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. Detective drama set in the north-east of England, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with a guest appearance by Jody Latham (Shameless)

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry and Nikki investigate a shooting at the Dutch embassy, where several people are killed and the ambassador's daughter Klara is wounded. Complications arise when orders are given to keep the identity of a dead bodyguard a secret, and an outside pathologist is appointed to explore the case. As Klara recovers in hospital, she gradually begins to remember who was responsible for the incident. Kate Ashfield (Collision), Lorcan Cranitch (Cracker) and Matthew McNulty (Lark Rise to Candleford) guest star

Rural drama, starring Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is hired to recover a priceless 16th-century samurai sword stolen from a ruthless businessman's house _ and he races to retrieve it before his old mentor gets to it. Ian McShane stars, with guest appearances by Julian Glover and Dinsdale Landen

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A firearms expert agrees to repair a valuable antique gun on Lovejoy's behalf, but unfortunately for all concerned, it is later used in a robbery. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Caroline Langrishe

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: The return of an old enemy leads to a series of problems, beginning with the loss of Sharpe's hard-earned captaincy, and culminating in a race against time to rescue his lover Teresa and their baby from the beleaguered city of Badajoz before Wellesley's forces attack. Military adventure, starring Sean Bean and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and his companions arrive on an abandoned spaceship, which allows them to travel back in time to 18th-century France. A little girl the Doctor befriends grows up to be Louis XV's mistress, and the time lord must discover why she is being pursued all her life by the ship's sinister clockwork robots. Sophia Myles guest stars alongside David Tennant and Billie Piper

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Tardis is unexpectedly thrown into a parallel universe. While Rose is amazed to learn her father is still alive in this alternative reality, the Doctor discovers a new generation of one of his old enemies, the Cybermen, is poised to take over the world. Roger Lloyd Pack guest stars

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Cybermen seize control of London and begin transforming the population into more of their kind. Jackie falls under Lumic's control, while the Doctor and his companions are forced to go on the run. As the mechanical villains' power grows, a desperate attack is mounted on the factory at the heart of their schemes. Roger Lloyd Pack guest stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy gets the chance to show off his talents as a concert promoter when Wesley almost runs over Billy Ingleton - a man who dreams of performing a piano recital. Christmas special from 1995, guest starring Norman Wisdom, with Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: David Jason stars in this follow-up to Roy Clarke's popular comedy Open All Hours. Once the dogsbody, Granville has inherited the business from his miserly uncle Albert Arkwright, but he is not alone in running the shop - he now has his own assistant in the shape of his son Leroy, who proves more popular with the ladies than his dad ever did. It's a typical day at the grocer's as Granville tries to get a date with old flame Mavis without her terrifying sister Madge finding out, while Leroy shuns the old delivery bike in favour of an altogether more attractive way to take out the orders. James Baxter and Lynda Baron co-star, with appearances by Johnny Vegas, Mark Williams, Brigit Forsyth, Nina Wadia and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: As the Brokenwood Golf Club gathers for its Wednesday morning round, a zombie-like figure lurches from the morning mist identified as Alison Stone, the wealthy club owner. Her face red and blistering, she collapses into a bunker and dies leaving Mike and Kristin to investigate an apparent homicide by poisoning. Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The clerical sleuth encounters a voodoo priest who is intent on winning back the woman he loves - no matter the cost. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An unknown tagger begins to paint graffiti around London, and claims to have been responsible for killing a celebrated street artist four years previously. Originally part of a gang, the victim went solo after receiving support from a wealthy art patron, and when the detectives reopen the case, they become suspicious of his resentful former crew members. Meanwhile, Brian signs up to a social networking site as TopCop999 to chart the gritty life of a lawman, much to his colleagues' displeasure

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony's incessant witticisms during an episode of Star Trek provoke a plot to humiliate him, which ends up forcing him to hide out in the water tank in the loft. Comedy, starring Neil Morrissey and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and his companions arrive on an abandoned spaceship, which allows them to travel back in time to 18th-century France. A little girl the Doctor befriends grows up to be Louis XV's mistress, and the time lord must discover why she is being pursued all her life by the ship's sinister clockwork robots. Sophia Myles guest stars alongside David Tennant and Billie Piper

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Tardis is unexpectedly thrown into a parallel universe. While Rose is amazed to learn her father is still alive in this alternative reality, the Doctor discovers a new generation of one of his old enemies, the Cybermen, is poised to take over the world. Roger Lloyd Pack guest stars

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Cybermen seize control of London and begin transforming the population into more of their kind. Jackie falls under Lumic's control, while the Doctor and his companions are forced to go on the run. As the mechanical villains' power grows, a desperate attack is mounted on the factory at the heart of their schemes. Roger Lloyd Pack guest stars

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Vic finds himself at the centre of a furore concerning the uncertain future of a promising rugby player and the situation at the Snow's reaches crisis point as it becomes clear that although four may be company, seven is most definitely a crowd. Rural drama, starring Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Ruth suffers the devastation of losing her baby as her pregnancy comes to an abrupt end, prompting husband Simon to turn to Peggy for support. Passionate feelings emerge between Alison and Stephen, and a medical crisis forces two elderly brothers to reach the conclusion that life's too short to be perpetually at each other's throats. Rural drama, starring Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

They also tell the story of a website racket that robbed people of millions of pounds, and Michelle finds out what's real and what's fake in the world of celebrity memorabilia

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob talks to the poet and playwright, who recently published his memoir My Name Is Why. He discusses what it was like growing up as the only black child in a sleepy market town outside Wigan in the 1970s, his time in care and how he was separated from his foster family at the age of 12 and left to fend for himself. He also returns to Ethiopia to find out more about his roots. Featuring contributions from colleagues including Steve Coogan, Benjamin Zephaniah, Linton Kwesi Johnson and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes a look at claims that diet drinks could be making people gain weight, Gloria Hunniford finds out whether all carbs should be avoided or if some are the key to a healthy lifestyle and Danny Crates looks at whether greasy and sugary foods affect people's skin

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martell Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit a terrace house in Stourbridge in the West Midlands, and in Greater Manchester a terrace house in Alkrington and a semi in Wigan. After each is auctioned, they talk to the buyers about their money-making plans

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Footage of near misses caught on CCTV. An experienced base jumper's parachute gets tangled up when he leaps off Beachy Head, passers by come to the aid of a fitness trainer collapsed in the street, and a fire near a petrol station causes staff and customers to run for safety. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: The future looks uncertain for some when money becomes a stumbling block, and a revelation rocks one family. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £500,000 budget find a character property with space for entertaining and a baby grand piano, as well as an outdoor setting to keep a keen gardener busy. Along the way, the presenter heads out to the oyster beds of Poole harbour to discover more about how they are farmed and harvested sustainably to meet popular demand.

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists head to Chester, where the buyer wants something to mark her 10th year of singing in an award-winning ladies choir. With £500 on offer, will it be a glass maker who works on a barge, a collage artist who re-works magazines, or a textile worker of wool who makes the buyer's day? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades examines the evolution of modern-day transport through archive footage and revisiting three repairs from the barn - a Penny Farthing bike, a tram seat and a speedometer

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four contenders battling for the last two places in the semi-finals, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champion Angellica Bell and finalists Les Dennis and Spencer Matthews. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders strive to remain in the reckoning

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this third edition, he takes a comedic sprint through British sport's greatest underdog moments, from Leicester City's incredible Premier League win in 2016 and Kelly Holmes' double Olympic triumph in 2004, to a certain Northern Irish snooker player with odd-looking glasses whose World Championship victory gripped the nation in 1985

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: John enlists the help of his Nan to reluctantly wait in for a parcel delivery, while Kayleigh wants to know where she stands with her colleague. Will they finally get together, or will his daydreaming soul mate look for a lift elsewhere? Sitcom, co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: With the arrival of baby Star, Jock struggles with his parental skills. Billy Murphy, meanwhile, comes up with a masterplan to carry out a heist to steal a consignment of high definition televisions and involves both Jock and Conor. However, drafting in young Star causes chaos. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Shane Casey

programme.title: The Shining (1980)
programme.desc: An aspiring writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubrick's horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on plant-based food, cooking a 'buddha bowl' with onion bhajis, with vegetable grower Bryde Marshall and actress Jane McCarry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures, which include West Ham United v Aston Villa and Leicester City v Manchester United

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Gabby Logan look back at some of the most memorable moments by British athletes at Olympic Games, counting down the top 25 moments from Britain's Summer Olympics. Some of the highlights expected to feature are the battle between Seb Coe and Steve Cram in the 1500m at Los Angeles 1984, where Daley Thompson also made headlines in the Decathlon. Steve Redgrave's attempt to claim a fifth rowing gold medal at Sydney 2000 is sure to be high up the list, as are Linford Christie and Sally Gunnell's exploits in the men's 100m and women's 400m hurdles at Barcelona 1992. London 2012 saw Team GB claim a record number of gold medals, with sporting luminaries such as Bradley Wiggins, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Andy Murray and Mo Farah proving why they were household names, and new stars such as Nicola Adams, Laura Trott and Anthony Joshua burst onto the sporting landscape

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair, with participants donating their fee to their chosen charities. Comedian Paul Chuckle, actress Amanda Henderson, comic and writer Geoff Norcott and chef and entrepreneur Levi Roots tackle questions on English football clubs, songs from animated Disney films, the Star Wars prequel trilogy and reggae music

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Olympics edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Anthony Ogogo, Helen Glover, Sir Matthew Pinsent, James Cracknell, Nick Skelton, JJ Chalmers, Jeanette Kwakye and Mark Foster

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: With Lev acting distant, Faith struggles to cope whilst waiting for the results of Luka's latest scan. In a crisis, she calls Dylan, asking to see a friendly face, and he agrees to be there to support her. Meanwhile, Ethan bumps into Fenisha at Holby Comic Con and the pair are delighted to see each other. However, things soon turn awry when a van is accidently driven into a merchandise stall, seriously injuring several people, and Ethan is suddenly struck by terrifying memories of last year's terrorist attack

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Iron Man 3 (2013)
programme.desc: The armoured avenger hunts for the mastermind behind an untraceable series of bombings, despite being cut off from his usual resources and traumatised by a recent brush with death. His search uncovers a secret conspiracy involving a drug that renders the user indestructible. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Gwyneth Paltrow and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Glaswegian geek Milan snoops around the bedrooms of potential partners, hoping to meet a woman who loves the great outdoors and shares his love of Star Wars and superheroes

programme.title: In the Dark Half (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager babysits a six-year-old neighbour, but is distraught when he unexpectedly dies in her care. While the dead child's father looks for someone to blame, she is tormented by the belief that his spirit is haunting the abandoned bunker she has made her refuge from her troubles. Horror, starring Jessica Barden, Tony Curran and Lyndsey Marshal

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Artists create pieces for buyers in Chesterfield who want something special to remember their beloved fathers and a stepdad, who they lost within the space of two years

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades traces heroic stories from the Second World War and how they still resonate today, revisiting three fixes from the barn; a watch, swagger stick and belt

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Walkers Crisps factory in Leicester, which he visited in 2016, finding out how they are coping during the coronavirus crisis. He speaks to manager Carl Lewis, who reveals that in order to keep up with demand they're making big changes to their output, switching away from single packets to a situation where 90 per cent of production is now the multipacks sold in supermarkets

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates another documentary revealing the survival techniques of the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. This film follows a young tree shrew as it darts through the rainforest of Borneo on the lookout for food. The creature's super-fast metabolism means it is always hungry, but as it searches for fruit, it must avoid fire ants, giant snakes and even a carnivorous plant. Cameras also follow a male chipmunk collecting acorns in preparation for winter - but while he's out, a bigger, more aggressive neighbour ransacks his store. Can he get the nuts back?

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Spanish Main (1945)
programme.desc: Dutch refugees shipwrecked on the coast of Cartagene in Spain are imprisoned by a villainous colonial governor. However, the leader of the group manages to escape and goes on to become a feared pirate captain. He then returns five years later to exact revenge. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Paul Henreid, Maureen O'Hara, Walter Slezak and Binnie Barnes

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman enables a celebrity guest to reminisce about one of their favourite holidays by taking them back to the area

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: A look back at three more days of the Rio Olympics, which saw Laura Trott and Jason Kenny claim their second and third gold medals of the Games respectively

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Michael Baggott assess items at Barrow Hill Roundhouse in Derbyshire and Paul Martin visits the village of Hathersage, which provided inspiration for Jane Eyre

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz show, presented by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland from Kuranda to Townsville, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He also visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Ellis family reflects on their time-travelling adventure, and explores how the legacy of 100 years of history lives on in the northern diet today. Lesley and Sara Cox visit a Liverpool bakery, and sisters Caitlin and Freya examine how recent changes on Manchester's Curry Mile affect peoples' evolving relationship with flavour. Finally Sara and northern chef Rob Owen Brown prepare a celebration meal for the family

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants do the make-up for London Fashion Week models, replicating the look demonstrated by Dominic Skinner - and with tight deadlines to meet, the pressure is on. As if that were not bad enough, fashion designer Henry Holland is the guest judge, helping to decide which two hopefuls will receive the 15-minute penalty in the next challenge, which involves creating a look based on a personal icon

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of 'radio activity', with Joe Lycett, Shazia Mirza, Josh Widdicombe and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Emily is getting excited about her favourite night of the year, Simon's annual pub quiz. However, when the evening arrives, Gandalf has a fall and cannot babysit, leaving her with a choice - miss the quiz or take Rosie along to the pub. In the early timeline, Emily goes back to work and puts Rosie into a nursery, only for the youngster to suffer a frightening medical emergency

programme.title: Frankie Boyle Live: Excited for You to See and Hate This
programme.desc: The comedian takes to the stage of the King's Theatre in his home town of Glasgow after completing his first major tour of Scotland in 12 years. Frankie turns his attention to a number of diverse subjects, including Glasgow's relationship with swearing and vitamins, British politics, his concerns about ageing, buying a dog and the nature of comedy itself

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Rupert's dream of passing on the family business to one of his children, resulting in 'the most humble day' of his life. In response to the revelations, the Murdoch Empire goes on the offensive and the alliance against him come under sustained pressure, but whistle-blowers begin to emerge, then a bombshell revelation completely changes public opinion. A sequence of disastrous events leaves Murdoch's business plans in ruins, and he closes the News of the World newspaper

programme.title: Alex Brooker: Disability and Me
programme.desc: The Last Leg presenter confronts what disability really means in Britain today. Alex is reunited with a childhood friend who has spina bifida, who challenges him to consider the impact of making jokes about his own disability. He also pays a visit to a bespoke disability helpline, meets members of Arsenal's disabled supporters club, and talks to Paralympic swimmer Susie Rodgers about her life

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The presenter reports on how southern Spain has become the main gateway for drugs into Europe, leading to violent turf wars between gangs and a government crackdown as the police struggle to regain control of their streets. Stacey goes on aerial patrol with police officers guarding the strip of water between Morocco and Spain, and discovers the extraordinary lengths smugglers will take to evade them, including travelling at 100mph in speedboats during extreme weather conditions

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A stone carver, a fine art painter and a heraldic artist pitch ideas for a piece of art to celebrate the 850th anniversary of a Scottish castle. Hosted by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The repairs of a cash register, a camera and a calculator, three pre-digital items that helped shape the modern computerised world people take for granted today

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: The final leg of Michael Portillo's journey begins in Edmonton, where he learns about the people who 300 years ago travelled huge distances by foot and canoe to trade with indigenous people. He also admires the city's early 20th-century streetcars, preserved by the Radial Railway Society, and its light rail system. Moving on, he views the Rocky Mountains, marvels at the scale of Canada's forestry industry and discovers how the beaver, once slaughtered for its fur, is now pampered

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on small creatures in an urban setting. In Rio de Janeiro, marmosets use power-lines as they would tree branches to scour the streets for food, and one animal becomes separated from its group and is forced to the ground, where it confronts the dangers of the city alone. Almost 11,000 miles away in the neon metropolis of Tokyo, a rhinoceros beetle begins an extraordinary journey to find sanctuary on a rare patch of greenery, along the way facing predatory bats, eight-eyed jumping spiders, praying mantises and a sharp-eyed crow. Including Hidden Kingdoms Revealed, showing how the film was made. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: One Touch of Venus (1948)
programme.desc: A statue of the Greek goddess of love is displayed in a department store, but when an employee impulsively kisses the sculpture, it comes to life. The immortal being inspires romance among everyone she comes into contact with, while the hapless man who summoned her is accused of art theft. Musical comedy, starring Ava Gardner and Robert Walker

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The Strictly Come Dancing judge takes model and presenter Melanie Sykes back to Llandudno in north Wales. Len surprises Melanie by picking her up in a Skoda 105 - just like the one her family had back in 1980. As they drive down the coast, the memories of a holiday she enjoyed in the area start flooding back. Plus, Mel provides an insight into her career, from modelling on the catwalks and appearing in a famous beer commercial, to presenting television shows

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: A final look back at the Rio Olympics, focusing on Britain's women's hockey team, Mo Farah in the 5,000m and Nicola Adams defending her London 2012 flyweight boxing title

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin visits the Watts Gallery in the heart of the Surrey countryside, where experts David Harper and Thomas Plant are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. David uncovers a collection of British film and TV memorabilia and Thomas is thrilled by some French cameo glass. Paul is on the trail of Martians in Woking, the setting for HG Wells's sci-fi novel The War of the Worlds

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Portillo continues his exploration of Queensland, from the tropical rainforest to the Great Barrier Reef. At Mungalla Cattle Station, Michael joins the owner for a feast cooked underground, and after a sing-along at the Billabong, he makes tracks for Townsville and the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef. Pioneering photographs taken at the time of his Bradshaw's brought the corals to the world's attention and now two million people visit the reef annually

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day one of the Third Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. The Windies may still be a long way from reproducing the quality of their incredible teams of the 1970s and 80s, but their new pace attack has been heralded as one of the strongest in world cricket, and should provide a great test for the English batsmen, whose time at the crease of late has understandably been limited

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals the progress of his tomatoes and gives advice on the summer maintenance of ponds. Nick Bailey travels to South Africa to search for the wild cousins of vibrant summer flower the crocosmia, while garden historian Advolly Richmond heads to Alton Towers in Staffordshire to take a close look at the Victorian trend of creating theme parks in their gardens. Plus, a second chance to meet a couple in Staffordshire who are very particular about the colours of the plants they use in the borders of their cottage garden

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: The Oxford-born actress, who became an Australian citizen six years ago, embarks on a 10,000km drive around the country discovering more about the place she can now call home. Starting in Bondi, she meets up with an estate agent who reveals the prosperous migrant market. Heading west, Miriam is disturbed to discover that there is a growing number of women who can't afford a home in retirement, now making up the fastest growing number of homeless in the country. With most of eastern Australia in the grip of severe drought, Miriam heads to the country town of Trundle, to see the repercussions for herself

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: A retrospective of a year when Boy George led the way in androgynous looks, Bananarama became the soundtrack to many a disco and Musical Youth passed the dutchie. Mouths were burned when sandwich toasters became the latest must-have kitchen appliance and the seedier side of student life was revealed in The Young Ones

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom has an awkward encounter, which affects his conversation with Louise about fading passion. Comedy, written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: For a Few Dollars More (1965)
programme.desc: An enigmatic gunslinger forms an uneasy alliance with a military veteran to track down a notorious outlaw - but tensions simmer as their very different motives for the hunt put them increasingly at odds. Sergio Leone's Western sequel, starring Clint Eastwood, Lee Van Cleef, Gian Maria Volonte, Josef Egger, Mara Krup and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation urged countries to stamp out infections as soon as they developed, but the UK government's initial scientific advice said the route out of the crisis was for most of us to catch the virus so we could develop 'herd immunity'. Dr Faye Kirkland investigates this approach to the Covid-19 pandemic, asking whether it was a dangerous gamble with people's lives or a sound scientific method. Faced with a growing backlash and warnings that the NHS was close to being overwhelmed, politicians denied herd immunity was the policy and within a few days Britain pivoted to a new strategy - lockdown

programme.title: Keith Haring: Street Art Boy
programme.desc: International art sensation Keith Haring blazed a trail through the art scene of 1980s New York and revolutionised the worlds of pop culture and fine art. When he was diagnosed with Aids in 1989, he asked writer John Gruen to write his biography, and the subsequent audio interviews form the basis of this profile. They are combined with archive footage and contributions from friends, critics and contemporaries, telling the artist's story from the sleepy Pennsylvania of his youth to the clubbing scene of New York and to the streets, where he made his instantly recognisable graffiti-like art

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Brother and sister Harry and Lilly conjure up a spellbinding extravaganza for their two mothers with a Witches and Wizards-themed wedding. Presenter Ed Petrie is joined by Harry Potter star Afshan Azad and Miss Drill from The Worst Witch to help the duo create a day of flying, fireballs and chocolate magic potions

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Odd animal transportation tales, including the poisonous frog helping to make air travel more safe and a convoy of caterpillars on the lookout for a new home

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends compete in an onion-eating competition in Newent

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Community on Solid Ground is a youth centre in Whalley Range. Before its current management took over the building the area didn't offer much for the youngsters living there and anti-social behaviour was very common. That has dropped by 73 per cent since Community on Solid Ground came along, the kids can hang out, play sports and have a place to go that means they aren't hanging around the streets. Sadly the facilities are very tired and they don't have areas they can play their beloved sport on, so the Dengineers are going to transform their outside space into a sports arena worthy of the Olympics. Presented by Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Ecuador in search of ocean giants and the biggest baby animal on earth.

programme.title: Britain's Most Sensational Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Piers Taylor challenge three backyard inventors to let their imaginations run wild and come up with unique and visually stunning designs, including a sweet-dispensing carousel, an automated breakfast maker and a giant robot

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part two of two. Conservationist Giles Clark continues his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. At the sanctuary, Mary the sun bear is now nine months old. Now growing in confidence, she is becoming extremely determined, and picks up a skill unique to a sun bear, jumping. Later, the sanctuary receives some shocking news when they discover there has been a break-in, and the distraught team have to come to terms with a theft

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: A journey along America's West coast. The programme examines how the landscape is shaped by the Pacific Ocean as well as powerful forces beneath ground, particularly along the San Andreas Fault. The programme also visits the warm and sheltered waters of the Sea of Cortez, where animals including blue whales gather, and discovers how a small community in America's westernmost point have a unique relationship with grey whales

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson presents a guide to Italian cookery, demonstrating how to create traditional and exciting meals using ingredients that are widely available in most supermarkets. She begins by preparing Sicilian pasta with tomatoes, garlic and almonds, a family feast of tagliata and Tuscan fries, and a fiery egg dish designed for late-night parties

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues a tired rocking chair, old scaffolding poles and a pair of dining chairs from the tip, enlisting Kev Paxton and Simion Hawton-Smith to help transform them

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is tasked with helping a couple and their young sons to quit London and make a new life and home in Derbyshire, and spends time with an artisan knife-maker

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall celebrates volunteers who help wildlife in the West Country, meeting a woman who has transformed her home into a hedgehog hospital

programme.title: Animals with Cameras
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan and a team of scientists learn more about various species through the use of animal bodycams. In Namibia, they help conservationist Marlice van Vuuren find out whether the three orphaned cheetahs she has raised have developed the skills needed to hunt for themselves in the wild, with close-up footage revealing how the cats choose their prey. On Kanowna Island off the south coast of Australia, Gordon discovers how the fur seal population manages to thrive and hunt in the surrounding shark-infested waters, and in South Africa, cameras gives an insight into the lives of wild baboons

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade along the way. This time, Giles and Monica help out at The Torridon in the Scottish Highlands, Britain's most northerly five-star hotel. Its need to balance a luxury stay with being self-sufficient has Monica collecting seaweed for the kitchen and helping to herd Highland cattle, while Giles learns to change toilet seats and joins the staff in their afternoon off - for a swim in the beautiful but freezing-cold loch

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Music festival goers descend on Glasgow, a street art mystery is revealed, and the oldest and youngest train cleaners on the station set to work

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga charts the history of the Liverpool house from 1945 to the present day. Young newlyweds moved into the attic at the end of the war, but then the house fell into rapid decline and after a family of seven stayed there, it was marked for demolition, narrowly avoiding the wrecking ball by a strange twist of fate. Abandoned, it was eventually rescued in the 1970s by an enterprising group of local activists and turned into a home for creative people. Finally, David meets the current owner Gaynor, who has restored the home to its former glory

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from day two of the Third Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. The Windies may still be a long way from reproducing the quality of their incredible teams of the 1970s and 80s, but their new pace attack has been heralded as one of the strongest in world cricket, and should provide a great test for the English batsmen, whose time at the crease of late has understandably been limited

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy explores how American history has been mythologised and manipulated by generations of politicians, writers and protesters. She begins by examining the American Revolution - a David-and-Goliath battle of men with high ideals taking on the might of the British Empire. But how much of America's founding story is based on fact?

programme.title: World Cup 1966: Alfie's Boys
programme.desc: David Jason presents a documentary about how Alf Ramsey recruited the England football team that won the world cup in 1966, and was able to unite the members into a world class side. Featuring archive footage of the team in action and contributions from Bobby Charlton, Jack Charlton, Jimmy Greaves, George Cohen, Tina Moore, Harry Redknapp, Terry Venables, Geoff Hurst and Gordon Banks

programme.title: Match of Their Day
programme.desc: A special episode of the programme dedicated to Jack Charlton, the World Cup winner and former Republic of Ireland manager, who died earlier this month at the age of 85

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: The first of three special editions of the archive music show focusing on hits from the 1960s, including songs by the Foundations, Julie Driscoll, Sandie Shaw and Procol Harum. Narrated by Mark Radcliffe

programme.title: Just Jim (2015)
programme.desc: A Welsh teenager leads a lonely life after being rejected by his only friend. He becomes fascinated by a mysterious American who moves in next door, and the new arrival offers him some advice on how to be cool. The youngster starts to develop the life he had always longed for, but begins to wonder if he was happier the way he used to be. Comedy drama, starring Emile Hirsch and Craig Roberts

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Paul Rose continues his journey by exploring the soft and rolling coastline of south Devon, taking a snorkelling trip around Burgh Island and hitching a ride on the Dartmouth to Paignton steam train

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lakeside view, surrounded by golden autumn trees

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: While continuing her efforts to set up Harriet and the vicar, Emma looks forward to meeting Frank Churchill at the Christmas party, but when he fails to show up she becomes the subject of Mr Elton's unwanted attention. Later, village gossip focuses on the arrival of Jane Fairfax and a piano she was given by a mystery admirer, leaving the matchmaker reluctant to believe Mr Knightley could have sent the gift. Drama, starring Romola Garai, Louise Dylan, Blake Ritson and Laura Pyper

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon explore Italy's capital in search of the generations of ordinary Romans who have left their mark on the city's culture and gastronomy. In the first of two programmes, Giorgio leads Andrew to some of his personal favourite districts including Garbatella, Italy's first 'garden suburb', which boasts vibrant market stalls and village architecture, where he introduces him to the simplest Roman food. In turn, Andrew introduces Giorgio to some of the most moving pictures by Caravaggio, in what was once the city's foremost church for poor pilgrims

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon conclude their exploration of Italy's capital. In the second of two programmes, as well as marvelling at the mosaics in the 12th-century Basilica di San Clemente, Andrew takes Giorgio to an ancient Roman schoolteacher's classroom. Then it's on to a true architectural and civic wonder - the vast Testaccio Slaughterhouse, where workers were once paid in offal. Travelling to the Palazzo Colonna, Andrew shows Giorgio the Beaneater by Carracci, a Baroque painting that makes an everyday subject extraordinary

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the artistic achievements of the Romans, tracing their progression from thieves and copycats to pioneers of the use of unflinching realism. Portraits from the Republic reveal how they captured lifelike images and immortalised figures such as Julius Caesar and Cicero using marble, bronze and paint. Alastair also looks at how Rome's first emperor, Augustus, used art as a tool to forge his dominion

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon tells the story of the Royal Collection's remarkable resurrection. Following its fortunes from Charles II through to the 18th century and the enlightened collecting of George III, cameras reveal some of its greatest treasures, including a jewel-encrusted salt cellar in the shape of a castle and a gold State Coach

programme.title: Bought with Love: The Secret History of British Art Collections
programme.desc: Art historian Helen Rosslyn continues to explore impressive country houses, focusing on the Grand Tour era of the 18th century when aristocrats filled their homes with masterpieces by 17th-century classical painters. She visits Holkham Hall in Norfolk to view the collection there, explores the legacy of the Dukes of Richmond at Goodwood House, and learns how the one-time Lord Egremont of Petworth House in Sussex patronised JMW Turner

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lakeside view, surrounded by golden autumn trees

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon explore Italy's capital in search of the generations of ordinary Romans who have left their mark on the city's culture and gastronomy. In the first of two programmes, Giorgio leads Andrew to some of his personal favourite districts including Garbatella, Italy's first 'garden suburb', which boasts vibrant market stalls and village architecture, where he introduces him to the simplest Roman food. In turn, Andrew introduces Giorgio to some of the most moving pictures by Caravaggio, in what was once the city's foremost church for poor pilgrims

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose sets off from Ladram Bay, heading for the chalk cliffs of Beer Head in South Devon. Here John Scott takes him underground on a tour of Beer Quarry caves, where until a few years ago limestone had been extracted for almost 2,000 years. Continuing along the coastal path to Charmouth, he learns about fossil hunting and ammonites and after enjoying the wild orchids in the ancient wildflower meadows of West Hay farm, begins the steep climb up to Golden Cap, the highest point on the path with stunning views along the Jurassic Coast

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the Top of the Pops archives, featuring performances by acts including the Foundations, Procol Harum, the Bee Gees, the Rolling Stones, and Dave Dee, Dozy, Beaky, Mick and Tich

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles, Bruno Brookes and Gary Davies present the edition first broadcast on Christmas Day. Performers include Bros, the Beautiful South, Jason Donovan, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, Mike and the Mechanics, Michael Ball, Michael Jackson, Simple Minds, Madonna, Kylie Minogue, Cliff Richard and Prince

programme.title: Rodney P's Jazz Funk
programme.desc: Rapper Rodney P Edwards reveals how the first generation of British-born black youngsters was inspired by the avant garde musical fusions of black America in the '70s to lay the foundations of modern-day multiculturalism by creating the first black British music culture with the Jazz Funk movement. He meets key musicians including Leee John, lead singer of group Imagination, and Kenny Wellington, founding member of funk band Light of the World, as well as DJ s Cleveland Anderson and Carl Cox, and writer Robert Elms

programme.title: Hot Chocolate at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of BBC performances and rare interview extracts celebrating British pop group Hot Chocolate and in particular its lead soloist Errol Brown. The band's top 10 hits from the 1970s and early 1980s are showcased, alongside rarely seen early performances and cult fan favourites

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's last full year as British prime minister contrasted with global political change, while in the music world Pete Waterman's final year of chart domination heralded the dawn of Madchester. Some female artists brought attitude and power to the year, while an unlikely duet between a '60s legend and an '80s icon proved a charttopper and Jive Bunny kept the less discerning happy. As far as Top of the Pops was concerned, Radio 1's old guard was stood down as a team of fresh-faced recruits helmed the show. Contributors include Jason Donovan, Lisa Stansfield, Shaun Ryder, Chris Rea, Marc Almond, Sharleen Spiteri, Jazzie B and Jenny Powell

programme.title: I Can Go for That: The Smooth World of Yacht Rock
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Katie Puckrik revisits a halcyon period of Los Angeles studio craft when studio-based artists such as the Doobie Brothers, Steely Dan and Hall & Oates produced the smoothest R'n'B and married it to adult themes about longing, aspiration and melancholy. In its day this music was never identified as a genre, but in the 21st Century, in a nod to its finely crafted nature, it's come to be known as yacht rock. Katie looks at the singer-songwriters of Laurel Canyon as well as soft rock pioneers such as the band America. Contributors include Jay Graydon, John Oates, Mark Ronson and JD Ryznar

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: The stories of music entrepreneurs Jimmy Iovine and Dr Dre continues. This instalment explores Jimmy's reputation as a fearless, talented and indefatigable producer and how he reached the West Coast following a successful collaboration with Patti Smith. He describes moving to Los Angeles to produce with Tom Petty and his secret relationship with Stevie Nicks. Dr Dre talks about provocative songs, such as Straight Outta Compton, which were shaped by the bitter race relations in LA, how NWA evolved into a force to be reckoned with, and a devastating personal loss that overshadowed his success

programme.title: Rodney P's Jazz Funk
programme.desc: Rapper Rodney P Edwards reveals how the first generation of British-born black youngsters was inspired by the avant garde musical fusions of black America in the '70s to lay the foundations of modern-day multiculturalism by creating the first black British music culture with the Jazz Funk movement. He meets key musicians including Leee John, lead singer of group Imagination, and Kenny Wellington, founding member of funk band Light of the World, as well as DJ s Cleveland Anderson and Carl Cox, and writer Robert Elms

programme.title: Hot Chocolate at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of BBC performances and rare interview extracts celebrating British pop group Hot Chocolate and in particular its lead soloist Errol Brown. The band's top 10 hits from the 1970s and early 1980s are showcased, alongside rarely seen early performances and cult fan favourites

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and John hires extra help for the lambing season, with New Zealander Emma helping a ewe to give birth to triplets and hoping the smallest and weakest one will survive. Martin invests in some hi-tech kit to alert him when his cows are calving and collects semen from his prize bull to sell, while Mel spends her birthday at a cattle auction

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actor Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who lived and painted on France's Cote d'Azur, renowned for its sunny climate. The first edition focuses on the relationship between modern art and the location's development as an international tourist haven. Cezanne, Monet and Renoir were captivated by the light and colour of the landscape in the 19th century and their resultant work advertised the region's beauty. Meanwhile, the area's vivid hues inspired Henri Matisse to adopt a new palette that set modern art on the route to abstraction

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: As the residents of Brizan gather on the beach for the annual surf party, a strange cloud suddenly falls over the ocean. When it retreats, 10 competing surfers have disappeared and only their empty and abandoned boards are pushed back by the waves onto the shore. Supernatural French drama, starring David Kammenos and Marie Dompnier

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The surviving surfers discover they are endowed with strange powers, while the cloud is about to appear again. Supernatural French drama, starring David Kammenos

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Having reigned over musical theatre for nearly five decades, Andrew Lloyd Webber's music has delighted millions worldwide in hit shows like Jesus Christ Superstar, Evita, Cats and Aspects of Love. In this programme, the composer chats to Alan Yentob about his autobiography Unmasked, a candid and confessional account of his early life and career up to the opening of The Phantom of the Opera in 1986

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and John hires extra help for the lambing season, with New Zealander Emma helping a ewe to give birth to triplets and hoping the smallest and weakest one will survive. programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors reveal the turbinates - three incredible bones in your nose that warm up your snot so you can breathe clearly

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson

Having excelled at her previous club, Jess was signed by Manchester City Women's Academy a year ago and after a break-out season, she has already been selected as part of the senior squad. Will she have what it takes to cope with the step up?

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Jermaine Johnson, who plays Wozza in Jamie Johnson, meets up with gifted footballer Ezra. Signed to a top academy at six years old, he regularly returns to the grassroots club in south London where he learnt his skills, in order to keep his competitive edge. Jermaine joins him for the final session before the new season starts, but will he be able to cope with the pressure and keep up with the high standards?

Stripped off his gadgets, DM struggles to adapt to life in the wilderness and, when a talking pinecone called Conni begins to offer him survival tips, he begins to question his own sanity. programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. They use the magic of television to get to the top of Wudang Mountain in China; taste Durian, the world's smelliest fruit, in Malaysia; head to Japan for some cosplay in Tokyo's Yoyogi Park; eat coconut curry for breakfast in India and take a tiny taxi called a Tuk-tuk for a treasure hunt challenge in Sri Lanka! Ed is joined on this bonkers road trip by Inel Tomlinson, Hacker and Dodge, Naomi Wilkinson, Ben Shires, Victoria Cook and Michelle Ackerley - there's stuff to do in Asia that is totally ace and it turns up All Over The Place!

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Children organise a wedding for their unmarried parents or step-parents. Best friends Bella and Chloe plan a wedding of theatrical proportions as they put on a Shakespeare-inspired wedding. Mum and dad have to perform some of Shakespeare's most famous works for their wedding guests, while dodging rotten veg if their performance isn't up to scratch. With thunderous transport, outrageous acting and royal guests - this wedding is a full-on Shakespearean drama.
