programme.desc: Je'naé is cooking her favourite meal of Jamaican saltfish and cabbage

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: The children have lots of fun playing tents but after witnessing a news story about families who are living in tents because they have lost their homes, Bella decides to take action and organises a clothes collection with the help of her friends, family and school

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: The young girl struggles to get to sleep, so her mum plays a song that helps her dream about the animated world of a white owl

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley goes to see lobsters farmed, while JB Gill discovers how computers help cucumbers to grow. Plus, how goats' milk is turned into soap

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In Peru, seven-year-old Angel and his cousin, Santiago, go on an expedition up a mountain. Meanwhile, in Canada, eight-year-old Rhys spends the day with his pet dog

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Sister and brother, Isla and Noah fancy a spot of gardening and along with Mum and Dad they visit a garden centre to buy some new flowers to plant

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo wants to be a grown-up so that he can drive cars, watch scary movies and do whatever he wants, but he learns that adults do not always have the freedom to play

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory Crawford visits Rhys and his pet cockerel Colin. Together they clean out the chicken coop and give Colin fresh food and water. Rhys is getting Colin ready to take to a local poultry show and Rory gets to go along too.

programme.title: Monty & Co
programme.desc: Monty wants to go to the moon, but Eddie creates a scary moon experience. Afterwards, Monty decides he'd rather stay on Earth

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Gurdy has hurt his leg, and is frightened to take off a plaster in case it hurts

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is tidying up his bedroom, while Justin and his friends and playing in a recycling band

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how bike bells make a ringing sound and how line markings are made.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo is excited to show Gran Gran around the city farm - it's been a few months since she was last there, and she can't wait to see Melanie the chick again. But when they arrive at the chicken enclosure, they can't find the little fluffy chick - where could she be?

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie and Doug enjoy their first playdate together with their friends. They try out their new ball pit, but Waffle cannot join in because he's not toilet trained and he has to find fun elsewhere

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne visits Jamie and his eight puppies and together they head off to the vet for their very first check up! Rory travels to a wildlife rescue centre to help their animal care team look after some very cute patients

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Ball. Babies and their grown-ups play with balls and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Pink Bird and friends don their dancing shoes, top hats and canes to take the stage

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: A greedy spider hopes to attend a series of feasts in the forest, but attaches its thread to four animals and ends up being pulled in different directions

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Mr Hammond has asked Andy to give a talk all about elephant shrews. Jetting off in his safari-mobile, Andy heads to the African Savannah in search of the tiny creature and discovers amazing facts about it. Along the way, he meets a dung beetle, comes face to face with a hungry monitor lizard and narrowly escapes a herd of stampeding animals

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Preschool drama series about a girl who works on her brother's market stall during the summer holidays. Molly meets a new stall owner, Alice, and shows her round the Big Hub. While Molly takes Alice for a drink in the Wobbly Coffee Pot, James and Mack bring Alice's boxes in to set up the stall, but James drops one of the boxes, smashing some of her pottery. Bob suggests making a mosaic out of the broken pieces, and he and the kids make a mosaic sign for Alice's stall

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie and Liam are looking for a large piece of paper to do their next big painting on, but when their parents are too busy in the Post Office to help and Grannie Island is unable to assist, the feisty seven-year-old calls at the Lady Artist's house in the hope she will know where to get one from

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady's old film reels get themselves in a tangle, so Sarah and Duck decide to recreate the footage. Animated adventures of a seven-year-old girl and her duck

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The children have to do a few chores before Charlie can watch his favourite TV show

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: It's tidying up day and Tee's friend Lily the Tiger comes round to play her favourite game; Surprise! But can they surprise Mo?

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small determines to invent something himself that involves puffballs, airfruit and three extremely acrobatic froglets as part of Granny's birthday celebrations. Narrated by Michael Palin

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka visits the garden to wash everyone's faces, including the tiny Pontipines

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if a daily glass of wine is a good thing and Chris Bavin asks if calorie counting is a sensible approach to weight loss

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a terraced house in Wigston, Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe, Kent, and a piece of land in Bolton, Lancashire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Near misses caught on CCTV, including a woman trapped in her loft conversion while a fire rages below her and the race to rescue a boy who has fallen off a promenade into the sea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide expert advice to two teams as the competition heads to the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge. Presenter Anita Manning shows of a Bargain Hunt favourite with a Cornish flavour

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Impending changes come hard for Ryan, Renee, Hazel and Shannon. A letter for Kam sparks excitement

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple look for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £450,000, and also visits the Royal Crown Derby factory to find out about the porcelain industry

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A stone carver, a fine art painter and a heraldic artist pitch ideas for a piece of art to celebrate the 850th anniversary of a Scottish castle. Hosted by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The repairs of a cash register, a camera and a calculator, three pre-digital items that helped shape the modern computerised world people take for granted today

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Land of the Giants
programme.desc: Sue Perkins narrates a documentary shining a light on the world of people who grow outsize vegetables, following them from March to August as they gear up for the first big competition of the season at the Vale of Glamorgan Agricultural Show

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob talks to the poet and playwright, who recently published his memoir My Name Is Why. He discusses what it was like growing up as the only black child in a sleepy market town outside Wigan in the 1970s, his time in care and how he was separated from his foster family at the age of 12 and left to fend for himself. He also returns to Ethiopia to find out more about his roots. Featuring contributions from colleagues including Steve Coogan, Benjamin Zephaniah, Linton Kwesi Johnson and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes a look at claims that diet drinks could be making people gain weight, Gloria Hunniford finds out whether all carbs should be avoided or if some are the key to a healthy lifestyle and Danny Crates looks at whether greasy and sugary foods affect people's skin

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martell Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit a terrace house in Stourbridge in the West Midlands, and in Greater Manchester a terrace house in Alkrington and a semi in Wigan. After each is auctioned, they talk to the buyers about their money-making plans

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Footage of near misses caught on CCTV. An experienced base jumper's parachute gets tangled up when he leaps off Beachy Head, passers by come to the aid of a fitness trainer collapsed in the street, and a fire near a petrol station causes staff and customers to run for safety. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: The future looks uncertain for some when money becomes a stumbling block, and a revelation rocks one family. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £500,000 budget find a character property with space for entertaining and a baby grand piano, as well as an outdoor setting to keep a keen gardener busy. Along the way, the presenter heads out to the oyster beds of Poole harbour to discover more about how they are farmed and harvested sustainably to meet popular demand.

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists head to Chester, where the buyer wants something to mark her 10th year of singing in an award-winning ladies choir. With £500 on offer, will it be a glass maker who works on a barge, a collage artist who re-works magazines, or a textile worker of wool who makes the buyer's day? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades examines the evolution of modern-day transport through archive footage and revisiting three repairs from the barn - a Penny Farthing bike, a tram seat and a speedometer

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four contenders battling for the last two places in the semi-finals, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champion Angellica Bell and finalists Les Dennis and Spencer Matthews. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders strive to remain in the reckoning

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen searches for clues about crime in Wales. He uncovers a story of canine cops, police badgers and vigilante ferrets, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this third edition, he takes a comedic sprint through British sport's greatest underdog moments, from Leicester City's incredible Premier League win in 2016 and Kelly Holmes' double Olympic triumph in 2004, to a certain Northern Irish snooker player with odd-looking glasses whose World Championship victory gripped the nation in 1985

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian must try to keep his temper in check as he becomes a lord's butler for the day and is challenged to look after the gentry at an exclusive dinner party

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: John enlists the help of his Nan to reluctantly wait in for a parcel delivery, while Kayleigh wants to know where she stands with her colleague. Will they finally get together, or will his daydreaming soul mate look for a lift elsewhere? Sitcom, co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: With the arrival of baby Star, Jock struggles with his parental skills. Billy Murphy, meanwhile, comes up with a masterplan to carry out a heist to steal a consignment of high definition televisions and involves both Jock and Conor. However, drafting in young Star causes chaos. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Shane Casey

programme.title: The Shining (1980)
programme.desc: An aspiring writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubrick's horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on plant-based food, cooking a 'buddha bowl' with onion bhajis, with vegetable grower Bryde Marshall and actress Jane McCarry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures, which include West Ham United v Aston Villa and Leicester City v Manchester United

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Gabby Logan look back at some of the most memorable moments by British athletes at Olympic Games, counting down the top 25 moments from Britain's Summer Olympics. Some of the highlights expected to feature are the battle between Seb Coe and Steve Cram in the 1500m at Los Angeles 1984, where Daley Thompson also made headlines in the Decathlon. Steve Redgrave's attempt to claim a fifth rowing gold medal at Sydney 2000 is sure to be high up the list, as are Linford Christie and Sally Gunnell's exploits in the men's 100m and women's 400m hurdles at Barcelona 1992. London 2012 saw Team GB claim a record number of gold medals, with sporting luminaries such as Bradley Wiggins, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Andy Murray and Mo Farah proving why they were household names, and new stars such as Nicola Adams, Laura Trott and Anthony Joshua burst onto the sporting landscape

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair, with participants donating their fee to their chosen charities. Comedian Paul Chuckle, actress Amanda Henderson, comic and writer Geoff Norcott and chef and entrepreneur Levi Roots tackle questions on English football clubs, songs from animated Disney films, the Star Wars prequel trilogy and reggae music

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Olympics edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Anthony Ogogo, Helen Glover, Sir Matthew Pinsent, James Cracknell, Nick Skelton, JJ Chalmers, Jeanette Kwakye and Mark Foster

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: With Lev acting distant, Faith struggles to cope whilst waiting for the results of Luka's latest scan. In a crisis, she calls Dylan, asking to see a friendly face, and he agrees to be there to support her. Meanwhile, Ethan bumps into Fenisha at Holby Comic Con and the pair are delighted to see each other. However, things soon turn awry when a van is accidently driven into a merchandise stall, seriously injuring several people, and Ethan is suddenly struck by terrifying memories of last year's terrorist attack

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Shane: For the Love of the Game
programme.desc: Documentary following Shane Williams as he attempts to build a life after rugby, going to work with the family firm and trying out other sports - and finding the more he tries to get away from the game, the more it gradually pulls him back onto the pitch

programme.title: Iron Man 3 (2013)
programme.desc: The armoured avenger hunts for the mastermind behind an untraceable series of bombings, despite being cut off from his usual resources and traumatised by a recent brush with death. His search uncovers a secret conspiracy involving a drug that renders the user indestructible. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Gwyneth Paltrow and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Glaswegian geek Milan snoops around the bedrooms of potential partners, hoping to meet a woman who loves the great outdoors and shares his love of Star Wars and superheroes

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if a daily glass of wine is a good thing and Chris Bavin asks if calorie counting is a sensible approach to weight loss

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood.

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate counterfeit buffalo mozzarella being sold on supermarket pizzas, and potentially dangerous hairdryers. They also tell the story of a website racket that robbed people of millions of pounds, and Michelle finds out what's real and what's fake in the world of celebrity memorabilia

programme.desc: An aspiring writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubrick's horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on plant-based food, cooking a 'buddha bowl' with onion bhajis, with vegetable grower Bryde Marshall and actress Jane McCarry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures, which include West Ham United v Aston Villa and Leicester City v Manchester United

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Gabby Logan look back at some of the most memorable moments by British athletes at Olympic Games, counting down the top 25 moments from Britain's Summer Olympics. Some of the highlights expected to feature are the battle between Seb Coe and Steve Cram in the 1500m at Los Angeles 1984, where Daley Thompson also made headlines in the Decathlon. Steve Redgrave's attempt to claim a fifth rowing gold medal at Sydney 2000 is sure to be high up the list, as are Linford Christie and Sally Gunnell's exploits in the men's 100m and women's 400m hurdles at Barcelona 1992. London 2012 saw Team GB claim a record number of gold medals, with sporting luminaries such as Bradley Wiggins, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Andy Murray and Mo Farah proving why they were household names, and new stars such as Nicola Adams, Laura Trott and Anthony Joshua burst onto the sporting landscape

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair, with participants donating their fee to their chosen charities. Comedian Paul Chuckle, actress Amanda Henderson, comic and writer Geoff Norcott and chef and entrepreneur Levi Roots tackle questions on English football clubs, songs from animated Disney films, the Star Wars prequel trilogy and reggae music

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Olympics edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Anthony Ogogo, Helen Glover, Sir Matthew Pinsent, James Cracknell, Nick Skelton, JJ Chalmers, Jeanette Kwakye and Mark Foster

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: With Lev acting distant, Faith struggles to cope whilst waiting for the results of Luka's latest scan. In a crisis, she calls Dylan, asking to see a friendly face, and he agrees to be there to support her. Meanwhile, Ethan bumps into Fenisha at Holby Comic Con and the pair are delighted to see each other. However, things soon turn awry when a van is accidently driven into a merchandise stall, seriously injuring several people, and Ethan is suddenly struck by terrifying memories of last year's terrorist attack

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Iron Man 3 (2013)
programme.desc: The armoured avenger hunts for the mastermind behind an untraceable series of bombings, despite being cut off from his usual resources and traumatised by a recent brush with death. His search uncovers a secret conspiracy involving a drug that renders the user indestructible. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Gwyneth Paltrow and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Glaswegian geek Milan snoops around the bedrooms of potential partners, hoping to meet a woman who loves the great outdoors and shares his love of Star Wars and superheroes

programme.title: In the Dark Half (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager babysits a six-year-old neighbour, but is distraught when he unexpectedly dies in her care. While the dead child's father looks for someone to blame, she is tormented by the belief that his spirit is haunting the abandoned bunker she has made her refuge from her troubles. Horror, starring Jessica Barden, Tony Curran and Lyndsey Marshal

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if a daily glass of wine is a good thing and Chris Bavin asks if calorie counting is a sensible approach to weight loss

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a terraced house in Wigston, Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe, Kent, and a piece of land in Bolton, Lancashire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Near misses caught on CCTV, including a woman trapped in her loft conversion while a fire rages below her and the race to rescue a boy who has fallen off a promenade into the sea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide expert advice to two teams as the competition heads to the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge. Presenter Anita Manning shows of a Bargain Hunt favourite with a Cornish flavour

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Impending changes come hard for Ryan, Renee, Hazel and Shannon. A letter for Kam sparks excitement

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple look for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £450,000, and also visits the Royal Crown Derby factory to find out about the porcelain industry

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A stone carver, a fine art painter and a heraldic artist pitch ideas for a piece of art to celebrate the 850th anniversary of a Scottish castle. Hosted by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The repairs of a cash register, a camera and a calculator, three pre-digital items that helped shape the modern computerised world people take for granted today

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate counterfeit buffalo mozzarella being sold on supermarket pizzas, and potentially dangerous hairdryers. They also tell the story of a website racket that robbed people of millions of pounds, and Michelle finds out what's real and what's fake in the world of celebrity memorabilia

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The story of a man who abandoned a financial career to pursue a childhood ambition to become a professional rabbit-catcher. Steven McGonigal rejects modern pest control methods such as gas or poisons as environmentally dangerous, instead favouring the traditional skills he learned during his childhood in Magilligan, which involve using ferrets and dogs to herd animals towards him, so he can humanely kill them

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob talks to the poet and playwright, who recently published his memoir My Name Is Why. He discusses what it was like growing up as the only black child in a sleepy market town outside Wigan in the 1970s, his time in care and how he was separated from his foster family at the age of 12 and left to fend for himself. He also returns to Ethiopia to find out more about his roots. Featuring contributions from colleagues including Steve Coogan, Benjamin Zephaniah, Linton Kwesi Johnson and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes a look at claims that diet drinks could be making people gain weight, Gloria Hunniford finds out whether all carbs should be avoided or if some are the key to a healthy lifestyle and Danny Crates looks at whether greasy and sugary foods affect people's skin

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martell Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit a terrace house in Stourbridge in the West Midlands, and in Greater Manchester a terrace house in Alkrington and a semi in Wigan. After each is auctioned, they talk to the buyers about their money-making plans

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Footage of near misses caught on CCTV. An experienced base jumper's parachute gets tangled up when he leaps off Beachy Head, passers by come to the aid of a fitness trainer collapsed in the street, and a fire near a petrol station causes staff and customers to run for safety. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: The future looks uncertain for some when money becomes a stumbling block, and a revelation rocks one family. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £500,000 budget find a character property with space for entertaining and a baby grand piano, as well as an outdoor setting to keep a keen gardener busy. Along the way, the presenter heads out to the oyster beds of Poole harbour to discover more about how they are farmed and harvested sustainably to meet popular demand.

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists head to Chester, where the buyer wants something to mark her 10th year of singing in an award-winning ladies choir. With £500 on offer, will it be a glass maker who works on a barge, a collage artist who re-works magazines, or a textile worker of wool who makes the buyer's day? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades examines the evolution of modern-day transport through archive footage and revisiting three repairs from the barn - a Penny Farthing bike, a tram seat and a speedometer

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four contenders battling for the last two places in the semi-finals, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champion Angellica Bell and finalists Les Dennis and Spencer Matthews. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders strive to remain in the reckoning

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this third edition, he takes a comedic sprint through British sport's greatest underdog moments, from Leicester City's incredible Premier League win in 2016 and Kelly Holmes' double Olympic triumph in 2004, to a certain Northern Irish snooker player with odd-looking glasses whose World Championship victory gripped the nation in 1985

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: The religious anthem One Day at a Time by Gloria remains the biggest hit in the Irish charts, so it is unsurprising that country music that speaks to believers has a genre all of its own. This edition meets some of the men of the cloth who love country music, from singing reverends to jiving priests

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: With the arrival of baby Star, Jock struggles with his parental skills. Billy Murphy, meanwhile, comes up with a masterplan to carry out a heist to steal a consignment of high definition televisions and involves both Jock and Conor. However, drafting in young Star causes chaos. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Shane Casey

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: John enlists the help of his Nan to reluctantly wait in for a parcel delivery, while Kayleigh wants to know where she stands with her colleague. Will they finally get together, or will his daydreaming soul mate look for a lift elsewhere? Sitcom, co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson

programme.title: The Shining (1980)
programme.desc: An aspiring writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubrick's horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation urged countries to stamp out infections as soon as they developed, but the UK government's initial scientific advice said the route out of the crisis was for most of us to catch the virus so we could develop 'herd immunity'. Dr Faye Kirkland investigates this approach to the Covid-19 pandemic, asking whether it was a dangerous gamble with people's lives or a sound scientific method. Faced with a growing backlash and warnings that the NHS was close to being overwhelmed, politicians denied herd immunity was the policy and within a few days Britain pivoted to a new strategy - lockdown

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the dealers visit Warwick, where items of interest include a massive silver ring used for dishes and a 1960s classic car. Fay Rutter is tempted to take up windsurfing

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five prison officers from the north east answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Highlights include a diver slapped by a stingray and a man who hits himself with an axe

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and her team of experts return to give more families an eight-week challenge to get fitter, eat better and save money on their shopping. First up are the Formstones from Pontefract in West Yorkshire, whose aim is to stop snacking, ditch the convenience foods and get their chaotic shopping habits under control

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Wales is the only country in the world with a designated footpath that stretches around its entire coastline. Sean Fletcher sets out to explore the entire 870-mile Wales Coast Path, which runs from Chepstow in the south to Queensferry in Flintshire in the north. His first leg takes Sean along the coast of north Wales and on to the island of Anglesey. Along the way searches for Britain's rarest toad, turns his hand to making a Bronze Age axe head and has a beach cookout using mussels and oysters caught fresh from the Menai Strait

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. The first edition celebrates snacks, visiting the Wagon Wheel factory in south Wales making millions of biscuits a year and the Melton Mowbray company responsible for an award-winning pork pie. Plus, how potatoes are taken from the field and turned into crisps in a matter of hours

programme.title: Madeleine McCann: The Hunt for the Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Three-year-old Madeleine McCann disappeared from her parents' holiday apartment in Portugal 13 years ago, a mystery that has gripped the world. Now German prosecutors believe they have a prime suspect, a convicted sex offender who was living near the apartments at the time. But why has it taken a decade for him to be linked to the crime? Julie Etchingham investigates a trail that has involved police forces from at least three European countries

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When dock worker Owen Thorne plunges from the roof of a multi-storey car park while picking up his daughter from her school prom, Vera immediately detects foul play. The team soon discovers a work dispute over the death of a young man, with everyone believing Owen was to blame, and the victim's father admits he wanted revenge - but his alibi holds. The mystery then continues when Vera spots an inconsistency in the statement Owen's wife gave to the police, suggesting she's not certain of her husband's fidelity

programme.desc: The eccentric detective and his confidant Dr Watson investigate the apparent resurrection of a criminal mastermind with links to the occult, who is carrying out a series of seemingly magical murders. The case is complicated by the reappearance of Holmes' duplicitous old flame Irene Adler. Action thriller, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Rachel McAdams, Mark Strong and Eddie Marsan

programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare dishes perfect for enjoying outdoors, including spicy crab cake rolls served with thousand island dressing, cheese and sweet potato slices, classic donuts with both sweet and savoury fillings, homemade ginger beer and tropical lollies

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef ventures into Salem on the east coast of the USA, a place made famous by the witch trials of 1692. Things get spooky as James visits on the approach to Halloween and he meets a love clairvoyant, and her dogs

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.35 King George VI Stakes from Ascot, which is supported by four other races. Plus, four races from York, including the 2.40 Skybet Stakes. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the show's most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Fox, Ladybug, Flower, Tree and Rottweiler perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara present the final of the obstacle-course challenge. Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage, with those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the junior version of the singing talent contest, giving youngsters a chance to achieve stardom. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: You Only Live Twice (1967)
programme.desc: James Bond is sent on a mission to uncover the truth behind the disappearances of Soviet and American space capsules before the mutually suspicious superpowers go to war. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Highlights include a diver slapped by a stingray and a man who hits himself with an axe

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and her team of experts return to give more families an eight-week challenge to get fitter, eat better and save money on their shopping. First up are the Formstones from Pontefract in West Yorkshire, whose aim is to stop snacking, ditch the convenience foods and get their chaotic shopping habits under control

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Wales is the only country in the world with a designated footpath that stretches around its entire coastline. Sean Fletcher sets out to explore the entire 870-mile Wales Coast Path, which runs from Chepstow in the south to Queensferry in Flintshire in the north. His first leg takes Sean along the coast of north Wales and on to the island of Anglesey. Along the way searches for Britain's rarest toad, turns his hand to making a Bronze Age axe head and has a beach cookout using mussels and oysters caught fresh from the Menai Strait

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. The first edition celebrates snacks, visiting the Wagon Wheel factory in south Wales making millions of biscuits a year and the Melton Mowbray company responsible for an award-winning pork pie. Plus, how potatoes are taken from the field and turned into crisps in a matter of hours

programme.title: Madeleine McCann: The Hunt for the Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Three-year-old Madeleine McCann disappeared from her parents' holiday apartment in Portugal 13 years ago, a mystery that has gripped the world. Now German prosecutors believe they have a prime suspect, a convicted sex offender who was living near the apartments at the time. But why has it taken a decade for him to be linked to the crime? Julie Etchingham investigates a trail that has involved police forces from at least three European countries

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: Sid is very surprised to find a well under his house. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield residents are up in arms over the noisy lorries rumbling through the village on their way to Jim Pinder's quarry, and David joins the protest, only to end up spending a night in the cells. Sheep are stolen from Lily Barton's land, and her neighbour promptly becomes the prime suspect - only for her farm to be set alight. Meanwhile, Bellamy strives to fan the flames of passion with Gina. Jean Alexander guest stars

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff and Linda's wedding day arrives, Sandra Fowler is determined to stop her son's wedding, and an argument between Kim, Frank and Dave at Home Farm ends in tragedy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally tells Kevin they should ask Rita for a loan, competition heats up to buy the garage, and Maureen receives some horrifying news from Curly and Raquel

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel tries to make Maureen see that she is better off without Reg. Derek and Mavis look into acquiring an allotment. Kevin makes a decision about buying the garage

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Angie persuades Robert to arrange an evening out with the lads _ but neither she nor Ida are prepared for the consequences

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield residents are up in arms over the noisy lorries rumbling through the village on their way to Jim Pinder's quarry, and David joins the protest, only to end up spending a night in the cells. Sheep are stolen from Lily Barton's land, and her neighbour promptly becomes the prime suspect - only for her farm to be set alight. Meanwhile, Bellamy strives to fan the flames of passion with Gina. Jean Alexander guest stars

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary's past catches up with her when an unwelcome visitor delivers an ultimatum, as secrets and rifts threaten the unity of the family. News about the running of Downton Hospital puts Violet on the warpath, while those below stairs find social changes are putting their futures in jeopardy and Daisy's good intentions at the Mallerton House auction have disastrous consequences. Final series of the costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when the body of missing 10-year-old Jodie Gaines is found in a skip. The pair immediately identify two main suspects - a taxi driver who claims to have dropped the girl off at her guitar lesson, and the teacher who swears she never arrived. The case then takes an unexpected turn, leaving Steel and Phillips with the toughest choices of their careers. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: It's Murray and Bailey this week, as Rachel travels to Bristol with the DCI, who is advising on a rape and murder case that bears similarities to one she worked on 13 years previously involving the deaths of four women. Back at home, meanwhile, the detective discovers her brother Dom has been prostituting himself in her flat - and also learns the charges against her ex-boyfriend Nick are being dropped

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is very pleased with himself when he closes the deal on a large export order for his firm

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers where the money for his birthday party came from. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve Crane's wayward mother Babs arrives in Aidensfield, having fallen out with her screenwriter boyfriend and grown tired of the London arts scene. She causes all manner of chaos when a spate of burglaries provides her with the ideal opportunity to put her self-defence skills into practice. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps has an amazing change in fortune. Rural drama, starring James Carlton

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: An ambulance races Dave to hospital, Frank stops Kim from seeing the patient, and Biff and Linda's wedding reception is interrupted as news about Dave filters through

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank offers to pay for Dave's funeral, but Ned refuses, and Biff and Linda confront Kim about what happened to Dave. Zak declares 1996 as one of the worst years of his life

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek uses a camcorder to record the transformation of the allotment. Jack's day at the races leads to the men winning big, and he knows what they should invest their money in

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is furious after hearing Martin has invested in a racehorse, Maureen is sick of everyone being supportive of her, and Vicky is stunned when Alec arrives to stay

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve Crane's wayward mother Babs arrives in Aidensfield, having fallen out with her screenwriter boyfriend and grown tired of the London arts scene. She causes all manner of chaos when a spate of burglaries provides her with the ideal opportunity to put her self-defence skills into practice. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps has an amazing change in fortune. Rural drama, starring James Carlton

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Plans for the wedding are gathering steam, but Lady Mary's meddling lands Carson in trouble with Mrs Hughes. Cora and Violet clash over plans for the hospital takeover, and Thomas's job search is proving disheartening. Mary comes up with a fresh idea that offers Anna new hope, prompting a trip to London, and Lady Edith faces challenges at the magazine although it's a problem closer to home that reaches breaking point. A day out for the family and servants ends in panic and leaves Robert facing a difficult decision

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP returns to his former post at the surgery after the departure of Dr Dibbs, agreeing to stay until a replacement is found. He persuades a reluctant Louisa to move into his house so he can help raise their son, and suggests they could all move to London together in the future. Martin then delivers the eulogy at his aunt Joan's funeral and speaks to her sister Ruth, who proves to be just as cantankerous as he is. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: New receptionist Morwenna Newcross (Jessica Ransom) gets off to a bad start after arriving late on her first day, and her hyperactive behaviour leads Martin to believe she is high on drugs. Ruth tries to deal with an intruder on the farm who has stolen the hub caps from her car, and the sleepless nights begin to take their toll on Martin and Louisa, who are running out of ways to comfort their constantly wailing baby. Guest starring Andrew-Lee Potts (Primeval)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks, Devlin and Chandler investigate after a man is found badly beaten in a park. They discover the victim, Roland Kirk - who lives in a nearby camper van - had many enemies in the community, but nobody is willing to talk. The case then takes a decisive turn when a local man capitulates after being arrested and points the finger at architect Harry Morgan, whose son had recently turned against him and aligned himself with Kirk. Kevin McNally guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The duo look into the murder of an eight-year-old boy, coming up against a paedophile who cruelly deceives his victim's family. Dom's HIV test comes back negative, so he sets out to celebrate - only to endanger his own life and jeopardise Rachel's sergeant's exam in the process. Janet tells Andy she is having second thoughts about their brief liaison, but he finds her decision hard to accept. Suranne Jones and Lesley Sharp star, with Liam Boyle, Nicholas Gleaves and Joe Duttine

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate a murder committed on the first night of a quiz weekend held at Chaucer College. Sealing the college grounds to interrogate the remaining 11 contestants, the pair discover the victim had a track record for sexual harassment involving fellow competitors, as well as past dealings with a shady engineering business funded by Russian gangsters and masterminded by Oxford professor Charles Milner - another quiz fan. With guest stars Alan Davies (QI), Timothy West (Bleak House) and Nicholas Farrell (The Iron Lady)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff uses his hospital radio show to talk about how he has forgiven Yasmeen and wants to help her. Abi moves into No 13, and when Adam spots Sinead's cardigan poking out of his bag, Daniel is embarrassed

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen phones Geoff with a surprising message, and Adam suspects that Gary has killed Rick. Nicky informs Daniel they could do more than talk for the money he's paying her

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen collapses on the first day of her trial, and Gary is concerned by Brian's interest in Beacon Woods because of a potential stash of Roman coins. Meanwhile, Ed worries that Aggie is working far too hard at the hospital

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The killing of a postman, whose doorstep deliveries often included more than just letters, rocks the idyllic rural hamlet of Goodman's Land and sends Barnaby on the trail of a murderer with vengeance in mind - if Sgt Troy can keep his eyes off the attractive village policewoman long enough to follow the clues. Murder mystery, guest starring Robert Glenister, with John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Gillian Kearney

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An arson attack on a Turkish social club leaves 17 people dead and Brooks, Devlin and Chandler fearing the crime was racially motivated. They uncover a trail of false identities, but face opposition from a respected barrister when they need to remove a key piece of evidence from a suspect's leg. Meanwhile, Steel is forced to confront one of his oldest friends. Guest starring Charles Kay, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A tragic communication error between officers leaves gay police constable Nick Bentley caught in the crossfire of armed drug dealers, with no back-up. Brooks and Devlin follow a lead to a small-time dealer who claims to have seen a second policeman standing nearby, doing nothing while his partner bled to death. The story shocks the detectives and their boss Chandler as they investigate a homophobic religious group at the centre of the incident involving members of their own force. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster leaves central Manchester, heading east along the Rochdale Canal. Not long into his journey, John finds himself struggling to contend with 19th-century technology at a cotton works. Further up the canal, he helps out members of an conservation group clearing the waterway of dumped supermarket trollies. After passing into West Yorkshire, it's on to Hebden Bridge, famous for the hard-wearing material corduroy, before John tries his hand at clog-making in one of Britain's few remaining factories dedicated to the craft

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: The doctor and his wife hire a servant, unaware that the lady in question is his long-lost mother

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine moves back in with her parents, Maureen heads to Lowestoft as she cannot cope not hearing from Reg, and Vicky and Steve visit Jim in prison

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Eric tells Curly that Reg has resigned from his job. Mike urges Sally to push Kevin harder to buy the garage. Maureen fears for Reg's safety

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric and Kathy reach a deal over the tearooms, Viv is nervous about going back to work at the post office, and Linda is scared when Chris pays her another surprise visit

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina resigns from working for Frank, Zak is fed up with playing Santa Claus, Biff's stag night gets underway at the wine bar, and Kim has a shock proposal for Dave

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious marksman sharpens up his sniper skills on a flock of unsuspecting sheep, before heading for Aidensfield and murdering Liz's ex-boyfriend, a newly arrived doctor. While she attempts to comfort the victim's bereaved spouse, Bellamy is shot in the arm and sets out in pursuit of the rifle-toting villain. Drama, with Jason Durr making his final appearance as Mike Bradley

programme.desc: New receptionist Morwenna Newcross (Jessica Ransom) gets off to a bad start after arriving late on her first day, and her hyperactive behaviour leads Martin to believe she is high on drugs. Ruth tries to deal with an intruder on the farm who has stolen the hub caps from her car, and the sleepless nights begin to take their toll on Martin and Louisa, who are running out of ways to comfort their constantly wailing baby. Guest starring Andrew-Lee Potts (Primeval)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks, Devlin and Chandler investigate after a man is found badly beaten in a park. They discover the victim, Roland Kirk - who lives in a nearby camper van - had many enemies in the community, but nobody is willing to talk. The case then takes a decisive turn when a local man capitulates after being arrested and points the finger at architect Harry Morgan, whose son had recently turned against him and aligned himself with Kirk. Kevin McNally guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The duo look into the murder of an eight-year-old boy, coming up against a paedophile who cruelly deceives his victim's family. Dom's HIV test comes back negative, so he sets out to celebrate - only to endanger his own life and jeopardise Rachel's sergeant's exam in the process. Janet tells Andy she is having second thoughts about their brief liaison, but he finds her decision hard to accept. Suranne Jones and Lesley Sharp star, with Liam Boyle, Nicholas Gleaves and Joe Duttine

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate a murder committed on the first night of a quiz weekend held at Chaucer College. Sealing the college grounds to interrogate the remaining 11 contestants, the pair discover the victim had a track record for sexual harassment involving fellow competitors, as well as past dealings with a shady engineering business funded by Russian gangsters and masterminded by Oxford professor Charles Milner - another quiz fan. With guest stars Alan Davies (QI), Timothy West (Bleak House) and Nicholas Farrell (The Iron Lady)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet
